home.heroAsideBody: Design and documentation before code. One flagship system at a time — architecture, contracts, and validation gates that match how serious platforms ship.
home.heroFooterNote: Premium depth without noise: one accent, disciplined grids, and motion that respects reduction settings — calibrated for hiring managers who have seen a thousand templates.

narrative.title: What this portfolio optimizes for
paragraphs: This site is not a trophy wall of logos. It is a narrow, high-signal lens: how systems are framed before code, how contracts stay honest when backends change, and how delivery stays sequential when scope tries to explode.

Flagship work and toolchain depth live on their own routes so you can skim the story here, then open the evidence where it belongs — case studies with problem, architecture, and stack; stack grouped by layer instead of a chaotic badge grid.
If that matches how your team builds, the next step is a conversation, not a keyword match.

teasersSection.title: Where the evidence lives
teasersSection.description: Home stays narrative. Catalogs and layer breakdowns stay on dedicated pages so nothing competes with the story.

flagship.title: Systems catalog
flagship.body: Four platforms in flight — each with problem framing, architecture, stack, and phase. Built like products, documented like serious engineering.

stack.title: Toolchain by layer
stack.body: Frontend, API, data, AI, and delivery — the actual tools used to ship and operate platforms, organized for scanning by role.

profile.title: Who I am
profile.description: Architecture-first engineer building large-scale platforms through owned studios — not slide decks.

flagship.title: Systems under active sequencing
flagship.description: Each build follows the same backbone: Next.js surface, service layer, Prisma, PostgreSQL, and optional Python AI where intelligence is justified.

stack.title: Stack and tooling
stack.description: A coherent toolchain for shipping typed, observable, maintainable systems — not a badge collage.

contact.title: Let’s build something serious
contact.description: Pick a channel. Each opens in its native surface so threads stay fast and searchable.
contact.body: Open to software engineering roles and selective platform engagements.
contact.subbody: Email is fastest for technical threads; LinkedIn for formal introductions.

hero.backgroundAlt: Abstract technology pattern representing system intelligence
